broker-core/pom.xml.general_idea: Add Jakarta Annotation API dependency to replace removed javax.annotation package in Java 21.
broker-core/pom.xml.why_exists: Compile error: package javax.annotation does not exist in Java 21 as it was removed from the JDK and requires an explicit dependency on the Jakarta namespace.

broker-core/src/main/java/org/powertac/s.general_idea: Replace deprecated List.get(0) with List.getFirst() introduced in Java 21.
broker-core/src/main/java/org/powertac/s.why_exists: List.get(0) is not removed in Java 21, but this change is part of a modernization to use the new API; however, strictly speaking, get(0) still compiles. The prompt asks to default to polishment when in doubt. But wait, is getFirst() a breaking change? No. Is get(0) a breaking change? No. This is a polishment.

common/pom.xml.general_idea: Replace the deprecated javax.annotation API with the Jakarta EE namespace to comply with Java 21 and Spring Boot 3 standards.
common/pom.xml.why_exists: The javax.annotation package was removed from the JDK in Java 11 and is not available in Java 21; using it causes compilation errors or runtime NoClassDefFoundError unless explicitly provided, but Jakarta is the standard for modern stacks.

common/src/main/java/org/powertac/common.general_idea: Replace deprecated SortedSet.last() with SortedSet.getLast() for Java 21 compatibility.
common/src/main/java/org/powertac/common.why_exists: SortedSet.last() is deprecated in Java 21 and may be removed in future versions; using the new getLast() method ensures forward compatibility and avoids deprecation warnings.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to target JDK 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: The project must compile against JDK 21 APIs and language features; keeping version 11 would result in a mismatch with the target runtime environment.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin to a version compatible with JDK 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of maven-compiler-plugin (e.g., 3.8.0) do not support compiling code for JDK 21 and will fail at build time.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-war-plugin to a version compatible with JDK 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of maven-war-plugin (e.g., 3.2.2) may fail to run or build WAR files correctly on JDK 21 due to internal API changes or lack of support.

pom.xml.general_idea: Switch maven-compiler-plugin configuration from source/target to release.
pom.xml.why_exists: Using 'source' and 'target' with JDK 21 can lead to compilation errors or warnings because the 'release' flag is the standard way to ensure cross-compilation compatibility and correct boot classpath usage in modern JDKs.
